title: RabbitMQ 4.3 Pub/Sub and Reliable Messaging with TypeScript 7
description: TypeScript backend developers who can build production HTTP services and need to design, implement, test, operate, and defend asynchronous RabbitMQ systems. This original, research-backed path uses repeated retrieval, cumulative artifacts, changed-case labs, stakeholder projects, and a performance exam.
outcomes: Demonstrate rabbitmq 4.3.2, amqp 0-9-1, node 24.18, npm 12, typescript 7, amqplib 2.0.1, architecture selection, exchanges, queues, bindings, connections, channels, heartbeats, recovery, topology, direct and fanout pub/sub, topic and headers routing, envelopes, asyncapi 3.1, cloudevents 1.0.2, publisher backpressure, returns, confirms, consumers, acknowledgements, prefetch, idempotency, retries, dead lettering, quorum queues, rabbitmq 4.3 delayed retry and priorities, ordering, streams, outbox and sagas, tls, authorization, opentelemetry 1.43, prometheus, capacity, testing, upgrades, and recovery through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.

Demonstrate runnable deterministic pure-typescript messaging decision functions plus explicit clean compilation and disposable rabbitmq, tls, partition, restart, load, upgrade, and production transfer gates through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.
Demonstrate five cumulative messaging systems and a performance-based production defense exam through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.

competencies.statement: Define producer, consumer, operator, security, accessibility, latency, loss, duplicate, and recovery evidence for one messaging outcome.
misconceptions: A successful publish call proves the user-visible workflow completed.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Define producer, consumer, operator, security, accessibility, latency, loss, duplicate, and recovery evidence for one messaging outcome.

competencies.statement: Distinguish commands, events, notifications, documents, and queries by ownership, audience, expected handling, and change semantics.
misconceptions: Every message is an event and can be named with a past-tense verb.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Distinguish commands, events, notifications, documents, and queries by ownership, audience, expected handling, and change semantics.

competencies.statement: State at-most-once, at-least-once, effectively-once effect, ordering, and durability claims without promising impossible end-to-end exactly-once delivery.
misconceptions: RabbitMQ can guarantee exactly one business effect across brokers, databases, and external APIs.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: State at-most-once, at-least-once, effectively-once effect, ordering, and durability claims without promising impossible end-to-end exactly-once delivery.

competencies.statement: Map message ownership from producer intent through local buffer, broker confirmation, queue, delivery, consumer acknowledgement, and durable effect.
misconceptions: TCP acceptance transfers durable responsibility to RabbitMQ.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Map message ownership from producer intent through local buffer, broker confirmation, queue, delivery, consumer acknowledgement, and durable effect.

competencies.statement: Plan browser models, TypeScript compilation, contract tests, disposable RabbitMQ integration, partition, restart, load, and production transfer gates.
misconceptions: A deterministic browser simulation proves broker replication and network recovery behavior.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Plan browser models, TypeScript compilation, contract tests, disposable RabbitMQ integration, partition, restart, load, and production transfer gates.

competencies.statement: Choose asynchronous messaging only when temporal decoupling, buffering, fan-out, failure isolation, or replay creates measurable value.
misconceptions: A broker automatically makes a system less coupled and faster.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose asynchronous messaging only when temporal decoupling, buffering, fan-out, failure isolation, or replay creates measurable value.

competencies.statement: Distinguish competing consumers on one queue from publish/subscribe using one queue per subscriber responsibility.
misconceptions: Adding more consumers to one queue broadcasts every message to all consumers.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Distinguish competing consumers on one queue from publish/subscribe using one queue per subscriber responsibility.

competencies.statement: Evaluate request/reply over messaging against ordinary HTTP using correlation, timeout, cancellation, failure ambiguity, and capacity evidence.
misconceptions: Message-based RPC removes synchronous dependency and timeout coupling.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Evaluate request/reply over messaging against ordinary HTTP using correlation, timeout, cancellation, failure ambiguity, and capacity evidence.

competencies.statement: Choose choreography or orchestration by workflow visibility, policy ownership, compensation, evolution, and failure diagnosis needs.
misconceptions: Choreography has no coupling because services do not call each other directly.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose choreography or orchestration by workflow visibility, policy ownership, compensation, evolution, and failure diagnosis needs.

competencies.statement: Compare RabbitMQ queues, streams, databases, job schedulers, and logs against ordering, retention, replay, routing, throughput, and operational constraints.
misconceptions: RabbitMQ queues are a durable event log and should retain all history indefinitely.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Compare RabbitMQ queues, streams, databases, job schedulers, and logs against ordering, retention, replay, routing, throughput, and operational constraints.

competencies.statement: Trace producers, connections, channels, exchanges, bindings, queues, consumers, deliveries, and acknowledgements without collapsing their roles.
misconceptions: An exchange stores messages until consumers become available.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Trace producers, connections, channels, exchanges, bindings, queues, consumers, deliveries, and acknowledgements without collapsing their roles.

competencies.statement: Use virtual hosts as separate topology and permission namespaces while recognizing they are not complete process or resource isolation.
misconceptions: Two virtual hosts guarantee CPU, memory, disk, and failure isolation from each other.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use virtual hosts as separate topology and permission namespaces while recognizing they are not complete process or resource isolation.

competencies.statement: Derive destination sets from exchange type, routing key, binding key, headers, and exchange-to-exchange bindings.
misconceptions: A routing key names the queue that must receive the message.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Derive destination sets from exchange type, routing key, binding key, headers, and exchange-to-exchange bindings.

competencies.statement: Prove unroutable, single-route, fan-out, and overlapping multi-route outcomes with an explicit topology table.
misconceptions: A successful exchange publish implies at least one queue accepted the message.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Prove unroutable, single-route, fan-out, and overlapping multi-route outcomes with an explicit topology table.

competencies.statement: Classify connection-level, channel-level, topology precondition, return, confirm, and consumer cancellation outcomes by recovery scope.
misconceptions: Every AMQP error requires terminating the process and rebuilding the entire connection.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Classify connection-level, channel-level, topology precondition, return, confirm, and consumer cancellation outcomes by recovery scope.

competencies.statement: Pin Node 24.18, npm 12, TypeScript 7, amqplib 2.0.1, AMQP 0-9-1, and RabbitMQ 4.3.2 as one reviewed compatibility contract.
misconceptions: Any RabbitMQ tutorial code works unchanged across client and broker major versions.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Pin Node 24.18, npm 12, TypeScript 7, amqplib 2.0.1, AMQP 0-9-1, and RabbitMQ 4.3.2 as one reviewed compatibility contract.

competencies.statement: Choose one amqplib promise or callback API and account for RPC methods, event emitters, callback-only confirms, and non-RPC methods.
misconceptions: Every amqplib method in the promise API returns an awaitable Promise.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose one amqplib promise or callback API and account for RPC methods, event emitters, callback-only confirms, and non-RPC methods.

competencies.statement: Treat delivered bytes, fields, properties, headers, and decoded payload as untrusted runtime data despite TypeScript declarations.
misconceptions: A TypeScript interface validates JSON parsed from a message body.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Treat delivered bytes, fields, properties, headers, and decoded payload as untrusted runtime data despite TypeScript declarations.

competencies.statement: Own connection, channel, return, drain, close, error, blocked, unblocked, cancellation, and handler-error events without unhandled process failure.
misconceptions: A surrounding async try/catch captures errors emitted later by EventEmitter listeners.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Own connection, channel, return, drain, close, error, blocked, unblocked, cancellation, and handler-error events without unhandled process failure.

competencies.statement: Prove clean install, strict type checking, deterministic tests, compiled artifact, connection configuration, and graceful teardown under changed inputs.
misconceptions: A local tsx run proves the production artifact and broker contract are reproducible.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Prove clean install, strict type checking, deterministic tests, compiled artifact, connection configuration, and graceful teardown under changed inputs.

competencies.statement: Use bounded long-lived AMQP connections and explain multiplexed channels, negotiated limits, names, TLS, and lifecycle cost.
misconceptions: Opening a new connection for every publish provides the safest isolation with negligible cost.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use bounded long-lived AMQP connections and explain multiplexed channels, negotiated limits, names, TLS, and lifecycle cost.

competencies.statement: Assign channel ownership by publisher or consumer lifecycle and prevent concurrent confirm, acknowledgement, and topology operations from corrupting assumptions.
misconceptions: Channels are stateless thread-safe handles that can be shared across all work.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Assign channel ownership by publisher or consumer lifecycle and prevent concurrent confirm, acknowledgement, and topology operations from corrupting assumptions.

competencies.statement: Choose a heartbeat timeout with network and flow-control evidence and distinguish heartbeat interval, TCP keepalive, and false-positive failure.
misconceptions: A one-second heartbeat timeout always detects failures faster without risk.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose a heartbeat timeout with network and flow-control evidence and distinguish heartbeat interval, TCP keepalive, and false-positive failure.

competencies.statement: Reconnect with bounded exponential backoff, jitter, cancellation, credential refresh, readiness, and a single recovery owner.
misconceptions: Every close should trigger an immediate recursive reconnect from every channel.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Reconnect with bounded exponential backoff, jitter, cancellation, credential refresh, readiness, and a single recovery owner.

competencies.statement: Recreate connection, channels, topology, confirm state, prefetch, consumers, and publisher admission in generation order while rejecting stale callbacks.
misconceptions: Once TCP reconnects, old channel references and consumer tags become valid again.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Recreate connection, channels, topology, confirm state, prefetch, consumers, and publisher admission in generation order while rejecting stale callbacks.

competencies.statement: Assign ownership for exchanges, queues, bindings, policies, migrations, rollback, and deletion across application and platform teams.
misconceptions: Every consumer should independently create and delete all topology it can see.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Assign ownership for exchanges, queues, bindings, policies, migrations, rollback, and deletion across application and platform teams.

competencies.statement: Make repeated declarations equivalent in type, durability, exclusivity, auto-delete, and immutable arguments and diagnose precondition mismatch.
misconceptions: assertQueue silently updates existing queue properties to the new options.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Make repeated declarations equivalent in type, durability, exclusivity, auto-delete, and immutable arguments and diagnose precondition mismatch.

competencies.statement: Choose durable, exclusive server-named, auto-delete, and queue-TTL lifecycles from responsibility and race evidence.
misconceptions: A non-durable named shared queue is the normal choice for temporary work in RabbitMQ 4.3.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose durable, exclusive server-named, auto-delete, and queue-TTL lifecycles from responsibility and race evidence.

competencies.statement: Place operator-changeable TTL, limits, dead lettering, delivery limits, and queue behavior in policies instead of immutable client x-arguments where supported.
misconceptions: Hardcoded x-arguments are preferable because applications can change them without queue recreation.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Place operator-changeable TTL, limits, dead lettering, delivery limits, and queue behavior in policies instead of immutable client x-arguments where supported.

competencies.statement: Migrate incompatible topology with versioned names, dual bindings, drain evidence, producer and consumer ordering, and rollback.
misconceptions: Redeploying all services simultaneously makes destructive queue changes atomic.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Migrate incompatible topology with versioned names, dual bindings, drain evidence, producer and consumer ordering, and rollback.

competencies.statement: Explain the default exchange queue-name binding and choose it only when direct queue-name coupling is intentional.
misconceptions: sendToQueue bypasses exchange routing and writes directly into queue storage.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Explain the default exchange queue-name binding and choose it only when direct queue-name coupling is intentional.

competencies.statement: Design direct exchange bindings that keep producer intent stable while consumer queues evolve.
misconceptions: A direct exchange can route only to one queue for each routing key.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Design direct exchange bindings that keep producer intent stable while consumer queues evolve.

competencies.statement: Use one queue with multiple consumers for load distribution and state that each delivery goes to one active consumer at a time.
misconceptions: Every active worker receives a copy of each queued task.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use one queue with multiple consumers for load distribution and state that each delivery goes to one active consumer at a time.

competencies.statement: Measure service time, prefetch, unacknowledged work, capacity, and tenant fairness instead of equating round robin with fair completion.
misconceptions: Round-robin delivery guarantees equal workload and completion latency.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Measure service time, prefetch, unacknowledged work, capacity, and tenant fairness instead of equating round robin with fair completion.

competencies.statement: Acknowledge only after a durable effect or safely repeatable handoff and retain message identity, attempt, result, and failure evidence.
misconceptions: A worker should acknowledge before starting slow work to prevent duplicates.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Acknowledge only after a durable effect or safely repeatable handoff and retain message identity, attempt, result, and failure evidence.

competencies.statement: Give each independent subscriber responsibility its own queue so every responsibility can receive and acknowledge its copy.
misconceptions: Multiple consumers on one queue implement publish/subscribe fan-out.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Give each independent subscriber responsibility its own queue so every responsibility can receive and acknowledge its copy.

competencies.statement: Use fanout routing when every binding should match and record the resulting copy count and storage responsibility.
misconceptions: Fanout exchanges ignore bindings entirely and deliver to every queue in the virtual host.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use fanout routing when every binding should match and record the resulting copy count and storage responsibility.

competencies.statement: Design exclusive server-named subscriptions for session-scoped state and account for disconnect, binding, declaration, and cleanup races.
misconceptions: A well-known auto-delete queue is always safe for many reconnecting client instances.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Design exclusive server-named subscriptions for session-scoped state and account for disconnect, binding, declaration, and cleanup races.

competencies.statement: Design durable subscription queues with retention, offline backlog, capacity, replay alternatives, and ownership limits.
misconceptions: A durable queue preserves every transient message across restart and unlimited subscriber downtime.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Design durable subscription queues with retention, offline backlog, capacity, replay alternatives, and ownership limits.

competencies.statement: State whether late subscribers miss prior events, receive retained queue backlog, or require a stream or database replay path.
misconceptions: A new fanout binding automatically receives messages published before it existed.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: State whether late subscribers miss prior events, receive retained queue backlog, or require a stream or database replay path.

competencies.statement: Design bounded dot-separated topic taxonomies whose stable semantic segments support exact, star, and hash matching.
misconceptions: Topic wildcards match arbitrary substrings inside each routing-key segment.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Design bounded dot-separated topic taxonomies whose stable semantic segments support exact, star, and hash matching.

competencies.statement: Enumerate overlapping topic bindings and prove which queues receive one copy even when multiple bindings on the same queue match.
misconceptions: A queue receives one duplicate for every matching binding from the same exchange.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Enumerate overlapping topic bindings and prove which queues receive one copy even when multiple bindings on the same queue match.

competencies.statement: Use headers exchanges only with typed bounded metadata and explicit all-versus-any matching, not as a schema-free routing escape hatch.
misconceptions: Headers exchanges validate message payload fields and ignore application headers.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use headers exchanges only with typed bounded metadata and explicit all-versus-any matching, not as a schema-free routing escape hatch.

competencies.statement: Route unmatched messages to an alternate exchange with observable ownership and distinguish it from mandatory returns.
misconceptions: An alternate exchange and mandatory publish produce the same publisher-visible outcome.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Route unmatched messages to an alternate exchange with observable ownership and distinguish it from mandatory returns.

competencies.statement: Use exchange-to-exchange bindings with bounded graph analysis, route evidence, permission checks, and cycle-safe topology review.
misconceptions: Exchange chains cannot create duplicate or cyclic routing behavior.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use exchange-to-exchange bindings with bounded graph analysis, route evidence, permission checks, and cycle-safe topology review.

competencies.statement: Bound message bytes, verify content type and encoding, decode once, parse safely, and keep malformed content out of domain effects.
misconceptions: Calling JSON.parse on Buffer content validates size, encoding, and schema.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Bound message bytes, verify content type and encoding, decode once, parse safely, and keep malformed content out of domain effects.

competencies.statement: Define message ID, type, schema version, source, subject, occurrence time, correlation, causation, tenant, and trace context without conflating them.
misconceptions: Correlation ID and message ID identify the same concept and can always be reused.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Define message ID, type, schema version, source, subject, occurrence time, correlation, causation, tenant, and trace context without conflating them.

competencies.statement: Validate unknown envelopes and payloads with strict runtime schemas, bounded collections, semantic refinements, and explicit rejection evidence.
misconceptions: Bundled amqplib TypeScript definitions make application message headers and bodies trustworthy.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Validate unknown envelopes and payloads with strict runtime schemas, bounded collections, semantic refinements, and explicit rejection evidence.

competencies.statement: Test producer and consumer compatibility for additive, optional, renamed, split, semantic, and removal changes across an overlap window.
misconceptions: Adding an optional field is always backward and forward compatible regardless of consumer behavior.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Test producer and consumer compatibility for additive, optional, renamed, split, semantic, and removal changes across an overlap window.

competencies.statement: Use AsyncAPI 3.1 and CloudEvents 1.0.2 selectively to document channels, operations, messages, bindings, examples, and event metadata while testing runtime truth.
misconceptions: A valid AsyncAPI document proves deployed routing and message behavior conform.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use AsyncAPI 3.1 and CloudEvents 1.0.2 selectively to document channels, operations, messages, bindings, examples, and event metadata while testing runtime truth.

competencies.statement: Treat amqplib publish and sendToQueue return values as writable-buffer admission signals rather than delivery confirmations or Promises.
misconceptions: A true return means RabbitMQ durably accepted the message.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Treat amqplib publish and sendToQueue return values as writable-buffer admission signals rather than delivery confirmations or Promises.

competencies.statement: Pause admission after false, coordinate a bounded drain waiter, handle close and abort, and avoid lost wakeups or listener leaks.
misconceptions: Every blocked publish should attach an unlimited permanent drain listener.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Pause admission after false, coordinate a bounded drain waiter, handle close and abort, and avoid lost wakeups or listener leaks.

competencies.statement: Propagate publisher capacity to HTTP or job admission with queue, byte, time, tenant, and rejection budgets.
misconceptions: Local memory can safely absorb any broker outage if requests are small.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Propagate publisher capacity to HTTP or job admission with queue, byte, time, tenant, and rejection budgets.

competencies.statement: Batch by count, bytes, age, confirm window, and cancellation while retaining each message result and fairness.
misconceptions: Larger batches always improve throughput without changing latency or loss exposure.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Batch by count, bytes, age, confirm window, and cancellation while retaining each message result and fairness.

competencies.statement: Respond to blocked and unblocked connection events, broker flow control, alarms, and local drain evidence without confusing their scopes.
misconceptions: A drain event proves RabbitMQ resource alarms and queue backlog have cleared.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Respond to blocked and unblocked connection events, broker flow control, alarms, and local drain evidence without confusing their scopes.

competencies.statement: Register return handling before mandatory publication and correlate returned content, exchange, routing key, properties, and publisher intent.
misconceptions: A mandatory return is thrown as a rejected publish Promise.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Register return handling before mandatory publication and correlate returned content, exchange, routing key, properties, and publisher intent.

competencies.statement: Distinguish broker confirms from consumer processing and combine mandatory returns or alternate routing with confirm results.
misconceptions: A positive publisher confirm proves a message was routed to at least one queue and processed.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Distinguish broker confirms from consumer processing and combine mandatory returns or alternate routing with confirm results.

competencies.statement: Build topology fixtures that assert expected destination sets, forbidden routes, unroutable cases, permissions, and changed bindings.
misconceptions: Counting bindings is enough to prove routing behavior for topic and headers exchanges.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Build topology fixtures that assert expected destination sets, forbidden routes, unroutable cases, permissions, and changed bindings.

competencies.statement: Classify unroutable messages as contract, deployment, tenant, or malicious failures and choose alert, quarantine, reject, or repair ownership.
misconceptions: Unroutable messages are transient and should always be retried with the same route.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Classify unroutable messages as contract, deployment, tenant, or malicious failures and choose alert, quarantine, reject, or repair ownership.

competencies.statement: Choose mandatory returns, alternate exchanges, or both from publisher feedback, broker custody, operational ownership, and observability needs.
misconceptions: Alternate exchanges provide the publisher the same immediate route-failure evidence as mandatory returns.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose mandatory returns, alternate exchanges, or both from publisher feedback, broker custody, operational ownership, and observability needs.

competencies.statement: Use confirm channels and account for callback results, waitForConfirms, nack, return ordering, close, and unresolved publication state.
misconceptions: waitForConfirms converts each publish call into an ordinary Promise-returning RPC.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use confirm channels and account for callback results, waitForConfirms, nack, return ordering, close, and unresolved publication state.

competencies.statement: Bound unconfirmed count, bytes, and age while preserving throughput, memory, fairness, and per-message outcome evidence.
misconceptions: Waiting synchronously after every publish is the only safe confirm strategy.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Bound unconfirmed count, bytes, and age while preserving throughput, memory, fairness, and per-message outcome evidence.

competencies.statement: Explain when confirms occur for unroutable messages, persistent classic queues, quorum queues, and multiple routed queues.
misconceptions: A confirm is issued as soon as bytes reach any RabbitMQ node socket.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Explain when confirms occur for unroutable messages, persistent classic queues, quorum queues, and multiple routed queues.

competencies.statement: Classify unresolved confirms after failure as ambiguous, retain stable message identity, and republish only through a duplicate-safe downstream contract.
misconceptions: No confirm means the broker definitely did not accept the message.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Classify unresolved confirms after failure as ambiguous, retain stable message identity, and republish only through a duplicate-safe downstream contract.

competencies.statement: Retire confirmed entries, reject nacked entries, preserve ambiguous entries, cancel waiters, and prove zero memory or callback leaks across channel generations.
misconceptions: The client library automatically retains a durable application ledger across reconnection.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Retire confirmed entries, reject nacked entries, preserve ambiguous entries, cancel waiters, and prove zero memory or callback leaks across channel generations.

competencies.statement: Report ready only after connection, topology, channel, prefetch, consume registration, and required dependencies are ready.
misconceptions: An open TCP connection proves the consumer can receive and safely process messages.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Report ready only after connection, topology, channel, prefetch, consume registration, and required dependencies are ready.

competencies.statement: Track consumer tags and channel generations so stale cancellation, delivery, and close events cannot mutate current state.
misconceptions: Consumer tags remain valid after a channel is recreated.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Track consumer tags and channel generations so stale cancellation, delivery, and close events cannot mutate current state.

competencies.statement: Handle a null delivery callback and cancellation notifications as lifecycle events with cause, readiness, recovery, and ownership evidence.
misconceptions: The delivery callback always receives a message object until the application calls cancel.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Handle a null delivery callback and cancellation notifications as lifecycle events with cause, readiness, recovery, and ownership evidence.

competencies.statement: Cancel new deliveries, then await, finish, nack, or abandon in-flight work under a deadline before closing the channel.
misconceptions: basic.cancel automatically requeues all deliveries already dispatched to the consumer.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Cancel new deliveries, then await, finish, nack, or abandon in-flight work under a deadline before closing the channel.

competencies.statement: Choose acknowledgement, requeue, dead-letter, or channel-close behavior for in-flight work during graceful and forced shutdown.
misconceptions: Acknowledging every in-flight message during shutdown prevents both loss and duplicates.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose acknowledgement, requeue, dead-letter, or channel-close behavior for in-flight work during graceful and forced shutdown.

competencies.statement: Choose manual or automatic acknowledgement from loss tolerance, consumer memory, work duration, and effect ownership.
misconceptions: Automatic acknowledgement means RabbitMQ waits for handler success before deleting the message.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose manual or automatic acknowledgement from loss tolerance, consumer memory, work duration, and effect ownership.

competencies.statement: Acknowledge, nack, or reject a delivery exactly once on its owning channel and prevent stale or cross-channel tag use.
misconceptions: Delivery tags are globally unique across a connection and survive channel recovery.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Acknowledge, nack, or reject a delivery exactly once on its owning channel and prevent stale or cross-channel tag use.

competencies.statement: Transfer ownership only after the intended durable effect, transactional inbox record, or safely repeatable handoff succeeds.
misconceptions: Acknowledging before a database commit improves safety because it shortens the unacked window.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Transfer ownership only after the intended durable effect, transactional inbox record, or safely repeatable handoff succeeds.

competencies.statement: Use redelivered and delivery-count evidence as hints while relying on stable application identity and durable deduplication for correctness.
misconceptions: redelivered false proves no consumer has ever seen the message.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use redelivered and delivery-count evidence as hints while relying on stable application identity and durable deduplication for correctness.

competencies.statement: Use one terminal delivery decision owner to prevent timeout, abort, handler, and shutdown paths from double acknowledging or conflicting.
misconceptions: Calling ack twice is idempotent and safely ignored by RabbitMQ.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use one terminal delivery decision owner to prevent timeout, abort, handler, and shutdown paths from double acknowledging or conflicting.

competencies.statement: Explain RabbitMQ per-consumer prefetch, deprecated global QoS, in-flight races, quorum caps, and the zero-means-unbounded hazard.
misconceptions: Prefetch controls the total number of ready messages stored in the queue.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Explain RabbitMQ per-consumer prefetch, deprecated global QoS, in-flight races, quorum caps, and the zero-means-unbounded hazard.

competencies.statement: Bound concurrent handlers by CPU, memory, message bytes, downstream pools, deadlines, tenant fairness, and shutdown capacity.
misconceptions: Setting prefetch alone limits every asynchronous task a handler may start.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Bound concurrent handlers by CPU, memory, message bytes, downstream pools, deadlines, tenant fairness, and shutdown capacity.

competencies.statement: Interpret consumer capacity with delivery rate, processing latency, ready and unacked depth, dependency saturation, and queue single-core limits.
misconceptions: Consumer capacity below 100 percent always means adding consumers will fix throughput.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Interpret consumer capacity with delivery rate, processing latency, ready and unacked depth, dependency saturation, and queue single-core limits.

competencies.statement: Change prefetch only with bounded measurement windows, hysteresis, safe limits, in-flight accounting, and rollback evidence.
misconceptions: Prefetch can be continuously maximized from one recent latency sample without instability.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Change prefetch only with bounded measurement windows, hysteresis, safe limits, in-flight accounting, and rollback evidence.

competencies.statement: Prevent one tenant or expensive message class from monopolizing consumers using topology, admission, bounded scheduling, and separate evidence.
misconceptions: One shared FIFO queue guarantees fair service across tenants and task costs.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Prevent one tenant or expensive message class from monopolizing consumers using topology, admission, bounded scheduling, and separate evidence.

competencies.statement: Derive a stable scoped operation identity from producer intent, tenant, message type, and semantic version rather than delivery metadata.
misconceptions: RabbitMQ delivery tags are suitable permanent idempotency keys.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Derive a stable scoped operation identity from producer intent, tenant, message type, and semantic version rather than delivery metadata.

competencies.statement: Record inbox identity, input fingerprint, state transition, domain effect, and completion atomically in one database transaction where possible.
misconceptions: Checking then inserting an inbox row in separate transactions prevents concurrent duplicates.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Record inbox identity, input fingerprint, state transition, domain effect, and completion atomically in one database transaction where possible.

competencies.statement: Reject reuse of an operation identity with a different payload fingerprint or semantic intent and retain conflict evidence.
misconceptions: Any later message with the same ID should return the first result even if its payload changed.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Reject reuse of an operation identity with a different payload fingerprint or semantic intent and retain conflict evidence.

competencies.statement: Use provider idempotency, effect ledgers, reconciliation, or compensation for non-transactional external effects and state residual ambiguity.
misconceptions: A local inbox transaction makes an external API call exactly once.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use provider idempotency, effect ledgers, reconciliation, or compensation for non-transactional external effects and state residual ambiguity.

competencies.statement: Set inbox retention from maximum redelivery, replay, audit, privacy, and disaster-recovery windows and test expired-key behavior.
misconceptions: Inbox records can be deleted immediately after acknowledgement because RabbitMQ will not redeliver.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Set inbox retention from maximum redelivery, replay, audit, privacy, and disaster-recovery windows and test expired-key behavior.

competencies.statement: Classify malformed, unauthorized, missing dependency, rate limit, timeout, conflict, transient, permanent, and ambiguous effect failures by evidence.
misconceptions: Exception class alone reliably determines whether a message should be retried.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Classify malformed, unauthorized, missing dependency, rate limit, timeout, conflict, transient, permanent, and ambiguous effect failures by evidence.

competencies.statement: Bound attempts, total age, delay, processing deadline, queue residence, and operator escalation as one retry budget.
misconceptions: A maximum attempt count alone prevents stale work and retry storms.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Bound attempts, total age, delay, processing deadline, queue residence, and operator escalation as one retry budget.

competencies.statement: Apply exponential backoff and jitter without blocking consumer slots or requeueing hot loops and preserve intended availability time.
misconceptions: Sleeping inside each consumer handler is an efficient delayed retry mechanism.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Apply exponential backoff and jitter without blocking consumer slots or requeueing hot loops and preserve intended availability time.

competencies.statement: Pause or shed broad consumer work when a shared dependency is unavailable instead of scheduling delayed retries for every message.
misconceptions: Returning every message with delay is always safer than pausing consumers during a total outage.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Pause or shed broad consumer work when a shared dependency is unavailable instead of scheduling delayed retries for every message.

competencies.statement: Record original identity, attempt, reason, first and next time, owner, budget, terminal disposition, and causal metrics without high-cardinality leaks.
misconceptions: Queue depth alone explains whether retries are healthy.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Record original identity, attempt, reason, first and next time, owner, budget, terminal disposition, and causal metrics without high-cardinality leaks.

competencies.statement: Trace nack or reject without requeue, expiry, length overflow, and quorum delivery-limit dead lettering through exchange and routing policy.
misconceptions: Deleting or expiring an entire queue dead-letters all of its messages.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Trace nack or reject without requeue, expiry, length overflow, and quorum delivery-limit dead lettering through exchange and routing policy.

competencies.statement: Configure dead lettering through mutable policy where possible and verify source read, target write, exchange existence, binding, and route permissions.
misconceptions: RabbitMQ creates a missing dead-letter exchange automatically when the first message fails.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Configure dead lettering through mutable policy where possible and verify source read, target write, exchange existence, binding, and route permissions.

competencies.statement: Detect and bound dead-letter cycles using history, stable identity, attempt policy, route analysis, and a terminal parking destination.
misconceptions: RabbitMQ always detects every dead-letter cycle and reports it to publishers.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Detect and bound dead-letter cycles using history, stable identity, attempt policy, route analysis, and a terminal parking destination.

competencies.statement: Account for RabbitMQ 4.x quorum delivery limit defaults, x-delivery-count, policy changes, group requeues, and intentional dead-letter retention.
misconceptions: Quorum queues retry poison messages forever unless an application counts attempts.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Account for RabbitMQ 4.x quorum delivery limit defaults, x-delivery-count, policy changes, group requeues, and intentional dead-letter retention.

competencies.statement: Give parked messages bounded retention, searchable redacted evidence, owner, repair, replay authorization, duplicate safety, and closure criteria.
misconceptions: Moving a message to a dead-letter queue completes incident handling.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Give parked messages bounded retention, searchable redacted evidence, owner, repair, replay authorization, duplicate safety, and closure criteria.

competencies.statement: Choose quorum queues for durable replicated data safety and reject them for high-churn temporary queues, lowest latency, or extreme backlogs better served by streams.
misconceptions: Quorum queues are the best default for every queue regardless of lifetime or workload.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose quorum queues for durable replicated data safety and reject them for high-churn temporary queues, lowest latency, or extreme backlogs better served by streams.

competencies.statement: Derive tolerated failures and availability from odd replica count, online majority, leader election, placement, and maintenance sequence.
misconceptions: A three-member quorum queue remains available after any two members fail.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Derive tolerated failures and availability from odd replica count, online majority, leader election, placement, and maintenance sequence.

competencies.statement: Combine persistent messages, publisher confirms, manual consumer acknowledgements, and duplicate-safe applications for quorum data safety.
misconceptions: Declaring x-queue-type quorum alone guarantees no loss or duplicates end to end.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Combine persistent messages, publisher confirms, manual consumer acknowledgements, and duplicate-safe applications for quorum data safety.

competencies.statement: Measure queue leader placement, client connection locality, cross-node routing, rebalance, and single-core hot-path constraints.
misconceptions: Clients must connect to the leader node or RabbitMQ rejects queue operations.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Measure queue leader placement, client connection locality, cross-node routing, rebalance, and single-core hot-path constraints.

competencies.statement: Plan majority loss, delayed confirms, redelivery, operator checks, replica repair, and residual data-risk evidence without unsafe force recovery.
misconceptions: Automatic reconnect immediately restores a quorum queue that permanently lost its majority.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Plan majority loss, delayed confirms, redelivery, operator checks, replica repair, and residual data-risk evidence without unsafe force recovery.

competencies.statement: Use RabbitMQ 4.3 quorum delayed-retry policies for returned or failed deliveries with explicit delay, eligibility, attempt, and compatibility evidence.
misconceptions: Delayed retry is enabled by default for every quorum queue in RabbitMQ 4.3.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use RabbitMQ 4.3 quorum delayed-retry policies for returned or failed deliveries with explicit delay, eligibility, attempt, and compatibility evidence.

competencies.statement: Compare native delayed retries with TTL and DLX cycles by rewrite cost, loss and duplicate windows, head-of-line effects, topology, and version support.
misconceptions: TTL dead-letter cycles and native delayed retry have identical safety and resource behavior.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Compare native delayed retries with TTL and DLX cycles by rewrite cost, loss and duplicate windows, head-of-line effects, topology, and version support.

competencies.statement: Use RabbitMQ 4.3 quorum 32 strict priority levels with bounded use, starvation evidence, prefetch interaction, and per-priority monitoring.
misconceptions: Strict priority guarantees low-priority messages eventually run under continuous high-priority load.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use RabbitMQ 4.3 quorum 32 strict priority levels with bounded use, starvation evidence, prefetch interaction, and per-priority monitoring.

competencies.statement: Align broker consumer timeout, handler deadline, dependency timeout, cancellation, redelivery, and channel recovery without duplicate terminal decisions.
misconceptions: A consumer timeout cleanly cancels application work and proves no external effect completed.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Align broker consumer timeout, handler deadline, dependency timeout, cancellation, redelivery, and channel recovery without duplicate terminal decisions.

competencies.statement: Gate 4.3-only behavior on deployed broker, queue type, policy support, client behavior, migration state, and tested fallback.
misconceptions: Compiling against amqplib 2.0.1 guarantees the connected broker supports RabbitMQ 4.3 features.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Gate 4.3-only behavior on deployed broker, queue type, policy support, client behavior, migration state, and tested fallback.

competencies.statement: State ordering scope across producer channel, exchange route, one queue, delivery, completion, redelivery, priority, and multiple consumers.
misconceptions: RabbitMQ preserves one global order across all publishers, queues, and consumer completion.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: State ordering scope across producer channel, exchange route, one queue, delivery, completion, redelivery, priority, and multiple consumers.

competencies.statement: Partition related messages by stable business key while bounding skew, hot keys, resharding, and cross-key independence.
misconceptions: Hashing a key across queues preserves order when partition count changes.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Partition related messages by stable business key while bounding skew, hot keys, resharding, and cross-key independence.

competencies.statement: Use single active consumer when one active delivery stream is required and account for failover, prefetch, recovery, and throughput limits.
misconceptions: An exclusive consumer and single active consumer provide identical failover behavior.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use single active consumer when one active delivery stream is required and account for failover, prefetch, recovery, and throughput limits.

competencies.statement: Defend state with entity version, sequence, monotonic transition, deduplication, gap detection, and reconciliation rather than transport order alone.
misconceptions: Discarding any message older than the latest timestamp safely resolves all ordering races.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Defend state with entity version, sequence, monotonic transition, deduplication, gap detection, and reconciliation rather than transport order alone.

competencies.statement: Measure throughput, tail latency, skew, recovery, and correctness when selecting queue count, consumer concurrency, and order scope.
misconceptions: Strict global order can scale horizontally without coordination cost.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Measure throughput, tail latency, skew, recovery, and correctness when selecting queue count, consumer concurrency, and order scope.

competencies.statement: Compare destructive queue consumption with retained stream offsets, replay, retention, protocol clients, and consumer responsibility.
misconceptions: Streams are simply queues that keep acknowledged messages a little longer.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Compare destructive queue consumption with retained stream offsets, replay, retention, protocol clients, and consumer responsibility.

competencies.statement: Define age and byte retention, segment behavior, start offset, consumer progress, replay authorization, and late-reader expectations.
misconceptions: A stream retains all messages forever unless every consumer acknowledges them.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Define age and byte retention, segment behavior, start offset, consumer progress, replay authorization, and late-reader expectations.

competencies.statement: Use super streams to partition throughput by key while accounting for partitions, routing, per-partition order, skew, and consumer coordination.
misconceptions: A super stream provides global ordering across all partitions.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use super streams to partition throughput by key while accounting for partitions, routing, per-partition order, skew, and consumer coordination.

competencies.statement: Account for stream protocol, replica locality, leader and follower reads, connection routing, confirms, and failure recovery.
misconceptions: Stream clients can use any cluster node with the same transparent routing as queues.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Account for stream protocol, replica locality, leader and follower reads, connection routing, confirms, and failure recovery.

competencies.statement: Select classic queue, quorum queue, stream, super stream, or external log using loss, latency, throughput, backlog, replay, routing, and operational evidence.
misconceptions: One RabbitMQ data type is always best if tuned with enough memory and prefetch.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Select classic queue, quorum queue, stream, super stream, or external log using loss, latency, throughput, backlog, replay, routing, and operational evidence.

competencies.statement: Enumerate database-first, publish-first, timeout, crash, confirm-loss, rollback, and duplicate failure windows for a dual write.
misconceptions: Publishing immediately after a database commit makes the two operations atomic enough.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Enumerate database-first, publish-first, timeout, crash, confirm-loss, rollback, and duplicate failure windows for a dual write.

competencies.statement: Store domain mutation and immutable outbox message identity, type, payload, destination intent, and availability atomically.
misconceptions: An outbox row written in a separate transaction closes the dual-write gap.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Store domain mutation and immutable outbox message identity, type, payload, destination intent, and availability atomically.

competencies.statement: Claim bounded outbox rows, publish with backpressure and confirms, mark completion safely, and recover ambiguous attempts through stable identity.

masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Claim bounded outbox rows, publish with backpressure and confirms, mark completion safely, and recover ambiguous attempts through stable identity.

competencies.statement: Combine consumer inbox deduplication, local domain effect, and outgoing outbox writes in one transaction to propagate effectively-once effects.
misconceptions: Inbox plus outbox provides exactly-once transport and removes all duplicates.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Combine consumer inbox deduplication, local domain effect, and outgoing outbox writes in one transaction to propagate effectively-once effects.

competencies.statement: Design saga state, commands, events, timeouts, idempotent steps, compensations, irreversibility, operator intervention, and audit evidence.
misconceptions: A compensation always restores the world to its exact state before the original action.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Design saga state, commands, events, timeouts, idempotent steps, compensations, irreversibility, operator intervention, and audit evidence.

competencies.statement: Use TLS with trusted CA, hostname and SNI verification, minimum protocol policy, client identity where required, and tested certificate rotation.

masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use TLS with trusted CA, hostname and SNI verification, minimum protocol policy, client identity where required, and tested certificate rotation.

competencies.statement: Choose generated credentials, x509 EXTERNAL, or OAuth 2 identity with explicit broker, client, token, renewal, and revocation support.

competencies.statement: Grant minimum configure, write, and read regex permissions per virtual host and separately evaluate topic authorization.
misconceptions: Read permission allows publishing and write permission allows consuming.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Grant minimum configure, write, and read regex permissions per virtual host and separately evaluate topic authorization.

competencies.statement: Load credentials from approved secret delivery, redact URLs and errors, update or reconnect under bounded rotation, and prove old access revocation.
misconceptions: Embedding credentials in an AMQP URL is safe because URL objects automatically redact passwords.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Load credentials from approved secret delivery, redact URLs and errors, update or reconnect under bounded rotation, and prove old access revocation.

competencies.statement: Defend tenant topology, message size, route, rate, queue count, headers, permissions, and noisy-neighbor budgets without trusting publisher metadata.

masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Defend tenant topology, message size, route, rate, queue count, headers, permissions, and noisy-neighbor budgets without trusting publisher metadata.

competencies.statement: Instrument publish, confirm, return, route, deliver, process, ack, retry, dead-letter, and effect phases with bounded identities and causal links.
misconceptions: One end-to-end span can stay open for every message until all subscribers finish.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Instrument publish, confirm, return, route, deliver, process, ack, retry, dead-letter, and effect phases with bounded identities and causal links.

competencies.statement: Interpret ready, unacknowledged, total, ingress, delivery, ack, redelivery, consumers, consumer capacity, connections, channels, and node resource metrics together.

masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Interpret ready, unacknowledged, total, ingress, delivery, ack, redelivery, consumers, consumer capacity, connections, channels, and node resource metrics together.

competencies.statement: Model arrival rate, service rate, concurrency, oldest age, burst, retry amplification, byte volume, and recovery time for capacity decisions.

competencies.statement: Trace memory and disk alarms, connection blocking, client blocked events, broker flow control, local publish drain, and upstream load shedding.

competencies.statement: Define availability, acceptance, routing, processing latency, age, loss, duplicate, poison, recovery, and capacity SLOs with symptom and cause alerts.

masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Define availability, acceptance, routing, processing latency, age, loss, duplicate, poison, recovery, and capacity SLOs with symptom and cause alerts.

competencies.statement: Test pure policy, envelope schema, topology routes, publisher confirms, consumer effects, disposable broker integration, and production probes at distinct layers.

competencies.statement: Inject return, nack, close, heartbeat loss, blocked connection, duplicate, poison, dependency timeout, majority loss, restart, and stale callback failures deterministically.

masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Inject return, nack, close, heartbeat loss, blocked connection, duplicate, poison, dependency timeout, majority loss, restart, and stale callback failures deterministically.

competencies.statement: Roll out schema, topology, publisher, consumer, policy, and broker changes in a compatibility-safe order with shadow evidence and rollback.

competencies.statement: Gate RabbitMQ 4.3 upgrade on supported 4.2 path, Erlang compatibility, feature state, alarms, replica health, client tests, rolling sequence, and rollback limits.
